Available online to all Worcestershire library members Access Cobra and sign in using your library card number An encyclopaedia of practical information for starting, running and managing a small business.

nodes.id: service::Great news for Malvern’s library members as Libraries Unlocked launches next week
nodes.label: Great news for Malvern’s library members as Libraries Unlocked launches next week
nodes.summary: Libraries Unlocked, the new service will go-live at Malvern Library on Monday 19 August 2024.
what_you_need: Libraries Unlocked is a new service which provides more flexibility for customers and community groups to use their local library at times that are convenient to them, between 8am and 8pm Monday to Saturday.
“Across Worcestershire, libraries are at the heart of community life, and I am glad that we are developing new approaches to accommodate residents' busy lives.
" Libraries Unlocked membership is for ages 15 and over.
Library members who wish to upgrade to Libraries Unlocked membership are required to complete a short induction.
To arrange a Libraries Unlocked induction visit Malvern Library and speak to a member of library staff or call 01905 822722 to make an appointment.

nodes.label: Hire a library meeting room

what_you_need: To do this you will need to apply directly to your local library in writing marking your envelope 'Art Application'.

nodes.id: service::Hop into Spring with Easter-themed activities at your local library!

nodes.summary: Looking for some egg-citing family fun this Easter? Worcestershire’s Libraries has you covered with a variety of cracking FREE activities for both the young and young at heart.

nodes.id: service::Improve Your Digital Skills at your Local Library
nodes.label: Improve Your Digital Skills at your Local Library
nodes.summary: Do you know someone with little or no experience using a computer? Worcestershire County Council’s Library service is here to help them build digital skills during Get Online Week.
what_you_need: We are committed to ensuring our local communities have the skills and confidence to access digital information and services safely and open new opportunities for personal and professional growth.
All our libraries provide free access to digital equipment, including desktop computers and printing facilities making them the perfect support centres for anyone needing digital assistance.

nodes.label: Libraries
nodes.summary: Discover an extensive collection of books and resources and a wealth of services and activities that encourage reading, learning and aspiration, improve skills and confidence and promote wellbeing and independence.
what_you_need: Your library membership Join the library, sign into your account, search the catalogue, renew and reserve books, pay fees and charges and book a computer.
Libraries Unlocked A new library service offering longer opening hours and more flexibility for customers and community groups to use their local library at times that are convenient to them.
Memories and Me Memory Bags are available to loan for carers and families of individuals living with dementia through the Memories and Me project.
Library strategy A vision for the future of Worcestershire Libraries explaining how library services are evolving to meet the changing needs of residents.

nodes.label: Libraries Unlocked
nodes.summary: A libraries unlocked membership allows customers to enter specific libraries outside of core staffed opening hours.
what_you_need: New technology allows customers with Libraries Unlocked membership to enter the library using their library card outside core staffed opening hours and to use library services independently when staff are not present.
Apply for a Libraries Unlocked membership Libraries Unlocked is live in Bromsgrove, Droitwich, Evesham, Kidderminster, Malvern, Pershore, Redditch, Rubery, St John's, Stourport and Tenbury Libraries.
how_to: visit one of the Libraries Unlocked sites during their core staffed hours - please check the opening times using the links below on our website before planning your visit
complete a brief induction with library staff - they'll guide you through the process and explain the operational procedures

nodes.id: service::Libraries Unlocked Launches Next Week at Redditch Library!
nodes.label: Libraries Unlocked Launches Next Week at Redditch Library!
nodes.summary: People in Redditch will soon have more freedom to use their local library at a time to suit them.
what_you_need: To continue enjoying full access to library services, including use of the building during extended hours, customers will need to upgrade their membership by completing a short induction.
“Providing greater access to libraries gives residents more freedom to use them in ways that suit their lives, and the initiative has already had a fantastic impact in other parts of the county.
”To arrange an induction, please visit Redditch Library and speak to a member of staff, or call 01905 822722 to make an appointment.

Customers have described Libraries Unlocked as: “A brilliant idea — it’s enabled us as a family to access the library more,” and “The best thing Worcestershire Libraries have done since the introduction of self-service.

nodes.id: service::Survey launched to help shape library services across the county

nodes.summary: Libraries are not only places to borrow books. They also provide vital community spaces for learning, connecting with others and supporting wellbeing. But what else could they offer?
what_you_need: Responses will help inform how library services are developed over the next two years, ensuring they continue to support residents, families and communities in the ways that matter most.

nodes.id: service::Sixth form and college library support

Your students will need digital library membership or a Worcestershire library card to use COBRA and complete the activities for this session.

nodes.label: Study Happy

what_you_need: Study Happy resource list Resources are available to help improve your studying, help you to have a healthy mindset and suggest some interesting ways to relax after a busy studying session.

nodes.summary: Work experience, volunteering and training placements are available at libraries throughout Worcestershire.

nodes.summary: Computer sessions are available on demand or can be pre-booked.

how_to: Receive notices only related to your loans, returns, reservations and important service updates.
Opt to also receive marketing updates on new library services, events and activities.
Remove all email permissions from your account - this option will stop all emails.

nodes.label: Join the library
nodes.summary: Library membership is free for everyone; join digitally now or visit your local library for full membership.
what_you_need: Become a library member The range of different library membership options are described below.

Digital library membership Worcestershire library members have free access to a wide range of digital resources including eBooks, eAudiobooks, eMagazines and subscription only E-resources, completely free of charge.
Instant membership gives you access to library computers and Wi-Fi for free and allows you to borrow 2 books.
All library members who are 15 and over are eligible to upgrade to free Libraries Unlocked membership.
Libraries Unlocked membership Libraries service behaviour guidelines and byelaws Worcestershire Libraries provides a safe and welcoming space.

nodes.label: Library Service at Home
nodes.summary: A volunteer run service that helps to ensure that everyone can continue to enjoy reading from the comfort of their own home.

nodes.label: Login to my library account
nodes.summary: Login to your library account to renew your books, reserve books, cancel reservations and change your PIN.
what_you_need: How to login To log into your account: you will need your PIN (personal identification number) in order to renew your books, log into your account or borrow eBooks.

If you want to sign up for, or no longer want to receive marketing e-mails from Worcestershire Libraries you can subscribe or unsubscribe by changing the marketing preferences in your library account.

nodes.label: Mobile library
nodes.summary: The mobile library visits 160 villages in Worcestershire every four to five weeks. It brings library services to customers who may be unable to travel to a static library.

nodes.label: Pay fees and charges
nodes.summary: Borrowing library books is free, but if you keep the books past their due date, late fees will apply. A full list of fees and charges can be found here.
what_you_need: Replacement library card charges If you have lost your library card, please visit a Worcestershire library and let a member of staff know and they can give you a replacement card.

nodes.label: Printing and photocopying services
nodes.summary: Worcestershire’s libraries offer convenient, self-service printing and photocopying at all our locations.
what_you_need: Libraries photocopying and printing service is a low-cost, accessible option for everyday printing or photocopying, whether you need a single page or a larger document.
Print Your Way is available to anyone with full library membership.
If you’re not yet a library member or you have a digital library membership only, it is easy to sign up for full library membership by visiting your local library and speaking to library staff.

nodes.id: service::Libraries ‘Go All In’ for the National Year of Reading 2026
nodes.label: Libraries ‘Go All In’ for the National Year of Reading 2026
nodes.summary: Libraries bring people together. Through stories, events and welcoming spaces, local libraries support people of all ages to discover, enjoy and share a love of reading.
what_you_need: In addition, libraries are one of the few places you can visit without any cost, as membership is FREE for everyone who lives in Worcestershire.

nodes.id: service::Library Christmas & New Year Opening Times
nodes.label: Library Christmas & New Year Opening Times
nodes.summary: Opening Times for Worcestershire Libraries across the festive period.

nodes.id: service::Library Christmas and New Year opening times
nodes.label: Library Christmas and New Year opening times
nodes.summary: Christmas and New Year Opening Times have been shared by Worcestershire Libraries.

nodes.label: Summer Reading Challenge
nodes.summary: The Summer Reading Challenge is the UK’s biggest free reading for pleasure programme for children.

nodes.label: Young Poet Laureate
nodes.summary: Worcestershire Young Poet Laureate is run jointly by Severn Arts and Worcestershire Libraries, celebrating the talented voices of young local poets.

nodes.id: service::Connect with others and boost your wellbeing
nodes.label: Connect with others and boost your wellbeing
nodes.summary: Improve health and wellbeing through opportunities to connect, inform and build confidence and resources that support self-help.
what_you_need: Readers Groups Informal groups of readers, who can connect socially while discussing books that have been borrowed from the Library.
Signposting Library staff are experts in supporting customer to access a range of information, resources and to source additional services that might be of help to them.

nodes.id: service::Libraries offer a free warm space to its residents this winter
nodes.label: Libraries offer a free warm space to its residents this winter
nodes.summary: As winter sets in, libraries across Worcestershire are here to provide everyone with a free, warm, and welcoming space.
what_you_need: Everyone is welcome to use the library as a warm space, and the friendly, knowledgeable staff are always on hand to ensure visitors feel comfortable and well-supported.

nodes.id: service::Stay warm and connected at your local library this winter
nodes.label: Stay warm and connected at your local library this winter
nodes.summary: As the colder months return, libraries across Worcestershire offer free, warm, and welcoming spaces for everyone in the community. Whether you’re seeking a quiet place to read or study an opportunity to take part in a library group or activity, or support with digital skills, job searching or setting up a business, your local library has something for everyone.

nodes.label: Warm welcome
nodes.summary: Worcestershire Libraries are part of the Warm Welcome Network which lists venues that can provide a warm welcome for those struggling to heat their homes this winter.
what_you_need: Libraries offer a wide range of free services to allow Worcestershire residents to stay connected: Welcoming and warm spaces to work, study, read and research or meet with friends.
Library membership is free and library members can borrow books download e-books, audiobooks, e-magazines, e-newspapers and search online reference sources for free.
Worcestershire’s 21 public libraries provide free access to computers, the internet and Wi-Fi and support from Digital Champions to develop IT skills and gain confidence to access online services.

nodes.label: Access to Research
nodes.summary: Millions of academic journal articles across many disciplines — for students and independent researchers.
nodes.what_you_need: Free walk-in use on a library computer (in-branch).

nodes.label: Ancestry
nodes.summary: Billions of historical records — census, births/marriages/deaths, military, immigration and more, spanning the 1500s–2000s.
nodes.what_you_need: Library membership. Ancestry Library Edition is normally used in the library / on library Wi-Fi — check the hub page for current at-home access.

nodes.label: Borrowbox
nodes.summary: Free eBooks & eAudiobooks — latest titles, award winners, non-fiction, classics and children's, plus the curated 'Worcestershire Reads' picks.
nodes.what_you_need: Library card number + PIN; choose 'Worcestershire' in the app.
access: Sign in with your card number + PIN.

nodes.label: British Film Institution (BFI Replay)
nodes.summary: Thousands of archive British films and TV programmes from the BFI National Archive.
nodes.what_you_need: Use on library computers / library Wi-Fi (in-branch).

nodes.label: COBRA (Business Support)
nodes.summary: Business start-up guides, market research and reference for new and growing businesses.
nodes.what_you_need: Library membership; supported by the Business & IP Centre (often used in-branch).

nodes.label: Digital library membership
nodes.summary: Instant free access to eBooks, eAudiobooks, eMagazines & eNewspapers and more.
nodes.what_you_need: Any Worcestershire resident — sign up online in minutes with your postcode. No card needed.

nodes.label: EBSCO
nodes.summary: Academic and reference databases — magazines, journals and research articles.
nodes.what_you_need: Library card + PIN; available at home.

nodes.label: ESPACENET
nodes.summary: Search 150+ million patent documents worldwide (European Patent Office).
nodes.what_you_need: Free for everyone; support via the Business & IP Centre.

nodes.label: Online events
nodes.summary: Live digital talks and workshops you can join from home.
nodes.what_you_need: Free — book a place via the events listing.

nodes.label: Oxford Dictionary of National Biography
nodes.summary: 60,000+ biographies of notable people from British history (Oxford DNB).
nodes.what_you_need: Free digital membership.

nodes.label: Oxford English Dictionary
nodes.summary: The complete Oxford English Dictionary — meanings, history and pronunciation.
nodes.what_you_need: Free digital membership.

nodes.label: Oxford Reference
nodes.summary: Thousands of dictionaries and reference works across every subject.
nodes.what_you_need: Free digital membership.

nodes.label: Oxford Research Encyclopaedias
nodes.summary: In-depth peer-reviewed research encyclopaedias across many fields.
nodes.what_you_need: Free digital membership.

nodes.label: Pressreader
nodes.summary: 7,000+ full-page newspapers & magazines, 60+ languages, 120+ countries.
nodes.what_you_need: Worcestershire library card number + PIN (free full membership, residents). No separate PressReader account needed.
access: Get the PressReader app (or use pressreader.com).
Sign in with your library card number + PIN.
At home you get a 30-day pass (re-confirm monthly); on library Wi-Fi it's 7 days.

nodes.label: The Times Digital Archive
nodes.summary: Every page of The Times newspaper, scanned back to 1785.
nodes.what_you_need: Free digital membership.

nodes.label: Theory Test Pro
nodes.summary: Practise the official DVSA driving theory test (car, motorcycle, LGV/PCV) including hazard-perception clips.
nodes.what_you_need: Library card; works at home.

nodes.label: Which?
nodes.summary: Independent product reviews and Best Buy buying advice.
nodes.what_you_need: Library membership (often used in-branch — check the page).
